questions.q: What kind of machine was PARAM 8000?
questions.explanation: PARAM 8000 was a supercomputer, widely regarded as India's first indigenously developed supercomputer.

questions.q: Which Indian organisation developed PARAM 8000?
questions.explanation: PARAM 8000 was built by the Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC).

questions.q: In which year was PARAM 8000 unveiled?
questions.explanation: PARAM 8000 was developed and demonstrated in 1991.

questions.q: PARAM 8000 launched which famous family of Indian supercomputers?
questions.explanation: PARAM 8000 was the first machine in C-DAC's long-running PARAM series of supercomputers.

questions.q: PARAM 8000 was developed largely because India was denied imported supercomputers from which country?
questions.explanation: The United States restricted exports of the Cray supercomputer to India, spurring an indigenous effort.

questions.q: What does the word 'PARAM' broadly signify, fitting the machine's role?
questions.explanation: 'PARAM' is a Sanskrit word meaning 'supreme', reflecting its status as a high-performance computer.

questions.q: C-DAC, the organisation behind PARAM 8000, is based primarily in which Indian city?
questions.explanation: C-DAC was established with its main centre in Pune, Maharashtra.

questions.q: PARAM 8000 is best described as which type of computing architecture?
questions.explanation: PARAM 8000 was a parallel-processing machine that combined many processors to achieve high performance.

questions.q: Building PARAM 8000 was significant because it demonstrated India's ability to do what?
questions.explanation: It proved India could design and build supercomputers on its own despite export restrictions.

questions.q: In which decade did India develop its first indigenous supercomputer, PARAM 8000?
questions.explanation: PARAM 8000 was developed in 1991, placing it in the early 1990s.

questions.q: C-DAC was set up in 1988 under which Indian government department/ministry?
questions.explanation: C-DAC was created in 1988 under the Department of Electronics (later part of the Ministry of Electronics and IT).

questions.q: Which computer scientist is widely credited as the key figure who led the development of PARAM 8000?
questions.explanation: Dr. Vijay P. Bhatkar, the founder-director of C-DAC, led the team that built PARAM 8000.

questions.q: Which family of microprocessors was used as the building block in PARAM 8000?
questions.explanation: PARAM 8000 was built around Inmos Transputer processors connected in parallel.

questions.q: The transputer-based design of PARAM 8000 reflected which broad approach to high-performance computing?
questions.explanation: PARAM 8000 used a massively parallel, message-passing design linking many transputer nodes.

questions.q: Roughly what peak performance class was PARAM 8000 designed to reach?
questions.explanation: PARAM 8000 was designed to deliver performance on the order of one gigaflop (a billion floating-point operations per second).

questions.q: Which US-restricted supercomputer was India seeking to import before deciding to build PARAM indigenously?
questions.explanation: India had sought a Cray supercomputer, but US export controls (citing dual-use concerns) blocked the deal.

questions.q: After PARAM 8000, C-DAC's PARAM line continued with which notable successor in the mid-1990s?
questions.explanation: PARAM 10000, delivering around 100 gigaflops, was a major successor unveiled in 1998.

questions.q: What was a key goal of developing PARAM 8000 beyond raw computing power?
questions.explanation: A central aim was technological self-reliance after India was denied access to foreign supercomputers.

questions.q: PARAM 8000 used which communication model among its many processing nodes?
questions.explanation: Its transputer nodes communicated by passing messages, characteristic of distributed parallel systems.

questions.q: Which of these scientific tasks is a typical application for a supercomputer like the PARAM series?
questions.explanation: Supercomputers such as the PARAM machines are used for weather forecasting, simulations, and scientific modelling.

questions.q: The success of PARAM 8000 helped position India among which group of nations?
questions.explanation: PARAM 8000 placed India among the small set of countries able to build their own supercomputers.

questions.q: C-DAC is best described as which kind of institution?
options: A premier R&D organisation for IT and electronics
questions.explanation: C-DAC is India's premier research and development organisation in advanced computing, electronics and IT.

questions.q: An early PARAM prototype was benchmarked at a 1990 international supercomputing show held in which city, where it ranked second only to a US machine?
questions.explanation: A PARAM prototype was benchmarked at the 1990 Zurich Supercomputing Show, where it came second only to a machine from the United States, drawing attention to India's capability.

questions.q: The transputers used in PARAM 8000 were designed by which company?
questions.explanation: The transputer was a parallel-computing microprocessor developed by the British company Inmos.

questions.q: A distinctive feature of the transputer architecture used in PARAM 8000 was that each chip had built-in what?
options: Communication links for connecting to other transputers
questions.explanation: Transputers integrated serial communication links on-chip, making it easy to wire many together into parallel machines.

questions.q: Approximately how long did C-DAC take to deliver PARAM 8000 after the project began, a notably short timeframe?
options: About six months only by accident
questions.explanation: C-DAC, founded in 1988, delivered PARAM 8000 by 1991, roughly a three-year development effort.

questions.q: Which programming/parallel-computing concept is central to extracting performance from a transputer-based machine like PARAM 8000?
options: Concurrency and parallel decomposition of tasks
questions.explanation: Performance depends on splitting work into concurrent tasks distributed across the parallel transputer nodes.

questions.q: Dr. Vijay Bhatkar, who led PARAM 8000, was later honoured with which of India's high civilian awards?
questions.explanation: Vijay Bhatkar was awarded the Padma Bhushan (and earlier the Padma Shri) for his contributions to supercomputing.

questions.q: Which later C-DAC PARAM supercomputer reached petaflop-scale performance, continuing the legacy begun by PARAM 8000?
questions.explanation: PARAM Siddhi-AI is a much later petaflop-class system in the PARAM lineage that PARAM 8000 began in 1991.

questions.q: The PARAM 8000 effort is often cited as a milestone in India's pursuit of which broader national policy goal in technology?
questions.explanation: PARAM 8000 became a symbol of India's drive for self-reliance in critical, export-restricted technologies.
